metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`, `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh`, and `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` to the edit-target list and update their `git commit` calls to include `-s` (or inject `Signed-off-by` via `git config trailer.*`) before the server flag flips.
findings.finding: The e2e test scripts `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` and `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh` / `feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` push commits to `refs/for/<test-branch>` WITHOUT a `Signed-off-by` trailer. Once `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` is set in `project.config`, these pushes will be rejected by Gerrit, breaking the live verification harnesses. The plan does not enumerate these scripts as consumers requiring updates.
findings.location: What section: 'any commit-authoring automation so agent commits comply BEFORE enforcement flips'; Scope IN: 'agent commit-convention update'
findings.suggested_fix: In each e2e script, add `git config trailer.sign.key 'Signed-off-by'` and set a responsible-human sign-off identity, then pass `--trailer 'Signed-off-by: <human> <email>'` to the commit commands, or simply add `-s` with appropriate `user.email`/`user.name` config matching the registered bot policy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `infra/gerrit/project.config` in the repo must be updated with `[receive] requireSignedOffBy = true` and pushed to `refs/meta/config` via `setup-project.sh` (the established declarative IaC path), rather than relying on a manual admin op against the live server.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`' and calls it an 'admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity', but does NOT explicitly state that `infra/gerrit/project.config` (the repo's declarative IaC file) must be edited and the change pushed via `setup-project.sh`. Every prior Gerrit config change in this codebase follows the declarative pattern: edit `infra/gerrit/project.config`, then run `setup-project.sh` to push `refs/meta/config`. If the implementer interprets 'admin op' as a manual SSH/UI change against the live server (without updating the repo file), the autodeploy's detect-only CONFIG_PATHS treatment will flag drift on the next run but never auto-correct it, and the next `setup-project.sh` invocation will overwrite the live enforcement flag with the un-updated repo file — silently disabling DCO enforcement.
findings.location: What section: 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config` for the rebar Gerrit project (admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity…)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, replace 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config` for the rebar Gerrit project (admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity…)' with 'Add `[receive] requireSignedOffBy = true` to `infra/gerrit/project.config` in the repo, then push it to `refs/meta/config` by running `setup-project.sh` (the established declarative IaC path used for all prior Gerrit config changes — see `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`). Requires admin SSH credentials (JoeOakhartNava identity).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact Signed-off-by trailer form for RebarBotNava bot commits in the plan (the human name string and registered email address that must appear), co-located with the agent sign-off policy — do not defer 'exact form decided and documented here' to implementation time.
findings.finding: The exact trailer vocabulary (name string and email address) for RebarBotNava agent/bot Signed-off-by commits is explicitly deferred — 'exact form decided and documented here' — but is never defined in the plan. An agent executor must produce a conforming 'Signed-off-by: Name <email>' trailer for every bot commit before the server flag flips; without a specified value vocabulary, the policy cannot be mechanically applied.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent/bot commits' paragraph: 'all RebarBotNava commits carry the sign-off of the responsible human using the registered bot email (exact form decided and documented here)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Agent/bot commits' paragraph, add a concrete example: 'Signed-off-by: Jane Smith <jane.smith@navapbc.com>' (or whichever human is the responsible operator) and state it explicitly, e.g.: 'All RebarBotNava commits carry the sign-off of the designated bot operator: Signed-off-by: <FullName> <registered-email@navapbc.com> — this trailer is added by [specific script/hook/tool].' Lock the form before drafting the CLAUDE.md and automation updates.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific scripts/hooks/tools that constitute 'commit-authoring automation' in scope (e.g. list each infra/gerrit/*.sh or other path that programmatically commits or pushes as RebarBotNava), so an executor has a concrete checklist rather than an open-ended search.
findings.finding: The plan instructs updating 'any commit-authoring automation' so agent commits comply before enforcement flips, but provides no inventory of which scripts, hooks, or tools constitute that automation, and no decision rule for how an executor should enumerate and verify them.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent/bot commits' paragraph: 'Update CLAUDE.md / CLAUDE.local.md commit guidance and any commit-authoring automation so agent commits comply BEFORE enforcement flips'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` stanza must be added to the repo's managed `infra/gerrit/project.config` file and applied via the existing `setup-project.sh` declarative path (not a one-off manual push), so it survives future config re-applies.
findings.finding: The plan describes the server-flag flip as an 'admin op' referencing the `JoeOakhartNava` identity, but does not specify whether the change flows through the existing declarative `setup-project.sh` / `infra/gerrit/project.config` management path (the established pattern for all prior `refs/meta/config` changes) or a one-off manual `refs/meta/config` push. The existing toolchain is strict about declarative, idempotent management — `setup-project.sh` overwrites the entire `project.config` on every run. A manual push that bypasses `setup-project.sh` would be silently reverted on the next `setup-project.sh` invocation (e.g. during a rollback or any future config update).
findings.location: What section: 'admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity per this checkout's credential setup; see gerrit config-project-config docs'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What section, replace 'admin op — requires the JoeOakhartNava identity' with: 'Add `[receive] requireSignedOffBy = true` to `infra/gerrit/project.config` in the repo and apply via `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` (the established declarative path for all refs/meta/config changes), so the flag is durable across future setup-project.sh runs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'agent tooling/guidance updated and verified' means operationally — specify a concrete check (e.g. 'a test push by RebarBotNava to refs/for/main carrying the Signed-off-by trailer is accepted by the current server config in a staging or dry-run environment') that must pass before the server flag is flipped.
findings.finding: The migration step 'agent tooling/guidance updated and verified' has no operationalized completion criterion — 'verified' is never defined as a concrete test, check command, or observable signal. An agent following the strict migration order has no decision rule for when this step is done and it is safe to proceed to flipping the server flag.
findings.location: What section — 'Migration order (strict): docs land → agent tooling/guidance updated and verified → server flag flipped → live verification'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3 — e.g. `git log --format='%H %s' refs/for/main | xargs -I{} git cat-file commit {} | grep -c 'Signed-off-by:'` against a test agent commit — so trailer presence is verified by artifact, not assertion.
findings.finding: AC3 ('agent-authored commits demonstrably carry the trailer before the server flag flips') lacks a concrete proving command: 'demonstrably' is a hedge that does not specify what artifact to inspect or what command produces evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Agent-commit sign-off policy documented in CLAUDE.md guidance; agent-authored commits demonstrably carry the trailer before the server flag flips.'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to: 'Verified by: create one agent commit via normal workflow, then run `git cat-file commit <sha> | grep -E "^Signed-off-by: .+ <.+@.+>$"` and confirm it matches the canonical form documented in CLAUDE.md; paste the sha and output as evidence on the ticket before flipping the server flag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4's process description with a verifiable artifact: e.g. 'Gerrit audit log (or git log on refs/meta/config) shows the `receive.requireSignedOffBy=true` commit timestamp is later than the CLAUDE.md update commit timestamp — paste both SHAs on the ticket.'
findings.finding: AC4 ('Migration order respected — no window in which agent/bot pushes fail') describes a one-time procedural transition with no proving command and no durable post-hoc verifiable state; it cannot be checked after the fact and is therefore not independently measurable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Migration order respected — no window in which agent/bot pushes fail (server flag flips last).'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to: 'Verified by: `git log --format="%ai %H %s" refs/meta/config | grep requireSignedOffBy` timestamp is later than `git log --format="%ai %H %s" HEAD -- CLAUDE.md | head -1` timestamp; paste both lines as evidence on the ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical bot commit trailer form explicitly in the plan (e.g. `Signed-off-by: Alice <alice@nava.team>` where Alice is the submitting human) so the CLAUDE.md update step is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The bot/agent sign-off form is deferred: the plan says 'exact form decided and documented here' for RebarBotNava commits, leaving the actual trailer content (email address, name string) unresolved at plan time. An executing agent cannot update CLAUDE.md or the commit-authoring automation without knowing the exact Signed-off-by string.
findings.location: What section — agent/bot commits bullet: 'exact form decided and documented here'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete example to the 'agent/bot commits' bullet: 'RebarBotNava commits MUST carry `Signed-off-by: <human-name> <human-email>` where the human is the engineer who approved the agent run; the bot is listed only as git author, never in Signed-off-by. Example: `Signed-off-by: Alice Nava <alice@nava.team>`.' Remove the parenthetical '(exact form decided and documented here)' placeholder.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction in the bot sign-off form: specify the exact `Signed-off-by:` trailer (name field = responsible human's real name, email field = responsible human's registered email — NOT the bot's email), consistent with the Kubernetes-2026 norm the plan itself cites, before the server flag flips.
findings.finding: The agent sign-off policy statement is mechanically contradictory: 'all RebarBotNava commits carry the sign-off of the responsible human using the registered bot email'. DCO's `Signed-off-by` trailer certifies the identity of the HUMAN certifying the contribution — it must carry that human's name and email, not the bot's email. A trailer of the form `Signed-off-by: Jane Developer <bot@example.com>` is not a valid DCO sign-off because the email does not identify the certifying human; the Linux kernel/Kubernetes-2026 norm the plan cites uses `Signed-off-by: Jane Developer <jane@example.com>` (the responsible human's real name AND email). The plan needs to clarify the exact trailer form before the approach is implementable.
findings.location: What section: 'all RebarBotNava commits carry the sign-off of the responsible human using the registered bot email (exact form decided and documented here)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'using the registered bot email' with 'using the responsible human's own name and email (e.g. `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joe@navateam.com>`) — NOT the bot's identity. The commit author remains RebarBotNava; the Signed-off-by certifier is the accountable human. Document the exact form in CLAUDE.md.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the CLAUDE.local.md guidance update should instead land in the tracked `CLAUDE.md` (or another tracked file), since `CLAUDE.local.md` is gitignored and cannot be committed/reviewed.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.local.md` is listed in `.gitignore` and is not a tracked repo file; it may not exist in any given checkout. The plan names it as a mandatory edit target for commit-guidance updates, but an executor cannot reliably edit an untracked, gitignored file in a durable, reviewable way.
findings.location: What section: 'Update CLAUDE.md / CLAUDE.local.md commit guidance'; Scope IN: 'CLAUDE.md/CLAUDE.local.md commit-guidance updates'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `CLAUDE.local.md` from the edit-target list in What and Scope; fold the agent sign-off policy entirely into the tracked `CLAUDE.md` commit-guidance section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure: document that reverting enforcement is accomplished by setting `receive.requireSignedOffBy = false` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config` and who has authority to do so, so ops can recover immediately if post-flip failures occur.
findings.finding: The plan prescribes a strict migration order before flipping `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` but provides no explicit rollback/back-out procedure if the enforcement flag causes unexpected failures after it is set (e.g., a bot identity gap or a mis-configured trailer template that wasn't caught in staging). T4 requires the reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of the breakage.
findings.location: What / Migration order section; Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to the Migration order: 'If post-flip failures occur, revert immediately by pushing a project.config with `receive.requireSignedOffBy = false` to refs/meta/config under the JoeOakhartNava identity. This is a one-minute operation. Re-investigate agent/trailer tooling before re-enabling.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback step to the migration order: document who can revert the `project.config` change, the exact revert command/procedure, and the criterion for triggering rollback if live verification fails.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no rollback procedure if the `project.config` flag flip causes unexpected breakage — for example, if signed pushes are incorrectly rejected due to trailer format differences, or if the Gerrit version does not honour the setting as expected. 'Live verification' is described (signed push accepted, unsigned rejected) but no remediation path is given for the case where verification reveals a problem after the flag is live.
findings.location: ## What — Migration order; AC: 'An unsigned push … rejected … and a signed push is accepted — both verified live'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the migration order: 'If live verification fails (signed push rejected or flag not enforced): revert by pushing project.config with the key removed via the same JoeOakhartNava identity; record the failure mode on the ticket before retrying.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a post-enforcement fix-up procedure for agent/bot commit failures to CLAUDE.md (or the agent sign-off policy doc): describe how the responsible human should amend the commit to add the trailer and re-push when an agent push is rejected by Gerrit.
findings.finding: The non-happy-path fix-up flow for agent/bot commits after server enforcement is live is not specified. The plan describes preventing agent failures via migration order, but once enforcement is live, if an agent commit is pushed without a valid trailer (e.g., tooling regression, misconfigured automation), the recovery procedure for the responsible human is absent.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent/bot commits' bullet; AC line 3 ('Agent-commit sign-off policy documented'); Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: In the CLAUDE.md/agent policy section, add a paragraph analogous to the CONTRIBUTING.md 'fixing DCO' Envoy-style note: 'If an agent-authored push is rejected for a missing Signed-off-by: the responsible human should run `git commit --amend --no-edit -s` (or `git rebase --exec "git commit --amend --no-edit -s" HEAD~N` for multiple commits) and re-push.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Establish a single term (e.g., 'automated/agent commits') and define its scope (all non-human commit authors, including RebarBotNava) in one place, then use that term consistently across What, Scope, and ACs.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'agent commits', 'bot commits', 'agent/bot commits', and 'RebarBotNava commits' interchangeably across sections without establishing that these always refer to the same set; AC3 uses 'Agent-commit' while the Scope says 'agent commit-convention' — a reader cannot confirm all usages are co-extensive.
findings.location: What section (Agent/bot commits paragraph), Scope IN list ('agent commit-convention update'), AC3 ('Agent-commit sign-off policy')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence definition early in 'What': 'Agent commits: any commit whose author is an automated identity (currently RebarBotNava; extend if new bots are added).' Then replace all variants with that term.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add the optional GitHub web-based commit setting explicitly to Scope OUT (if intentionally skipped) or add a lightweight AC covering it, so implementers and reviewers have a clear disposition.
findings.finding: The optional GitHub 'require sign-off on web-based commits' action is described in the 'What' section but is absent from both the Scope (neither IN nor OUT) and from the Acceptance Criteria — leaving implementers with no verification target and no clear decision on whether it is a deliverable.
findings.location: What section ('Optionally flip GitHub\'s...') and Scope section and Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Move 'Optionally flip GitHub\'s require sign-off on web-based commits' to Scope OUT with a one-line rationale ('mirror is read-only; deferring'), or add an AC: '[ ] GitHub mirror\'s require-sign-off-on-web-commits toggle confirmed flipped (or explicitly deferred with rationale recorded).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the unresolvable 'Kubernetes-2026 norm' citation with the actual rationale for the agent sign-off policy (e.g. 'the responsible human is the legal/moral certifier of DCO compliance; AI tooling cannot make the DCO attestation on its own behalf') — or cite a real, published, currently-existing community policy document.
findings.finding: The plan invokes 'the Kubernetes-2026 norm' as the authoritative basis for the agent/bot commit sign-off policy, but 'Kubernetes-2026' is a forward-dated, unverifiable reference — no such published Kubernetes policy document exists today. Citing a fabricated/future authority label to justify a design choice is a cargo-cult symptom: the policy itself (responsible human signs off; AI is not a co-author) is sound, but it doesn't need an unresolvable upstream norm to validate it.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent/bot commits — adopt the Kubernetes-2026 norm'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'adopt the Kubernetes-2026 norm (the responsible HUMAN signs off; AI is never a co-author)' to 'adopt the policy that the responsible human always signs off on agent-authored commits (AI tooling cannot make the DCO attestation on its own behalf; the human directing the agent bears the certification)' — dropping the unverifiable external authority citation entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether GitHub PRs are actually bot-closed automatically (no evidence found in .github/workflows); if not, restate the probot/dco exclusion rationale on accurate grounds (e.g., 'Gerrit is the enforcement point; probot/dco on GitHub PRs is redundant even if PRs remain open').
findings.finding: The Scope section excludes probot/dco with the rationale 'it watches PRs, which we bot-close — dead config.' The claim that GitHub PRs are 'bot-closed' is an empirically-checkable codebase assertion (trigger frame: 'we bot-close'), but no bot-close automation is present in the repository. A search of all .github/workflows finds no PR-close action; the only PR handling is a PR template that redirects contributors to Gerrit. If PRs are NOT bot-closed, the rationale for excluding probot/dco is unsupported — and if someone later installs probot/dco assuming 'bot-close makes it dead config,' they would find the app active on open PRs.
findings.location: Scope section — OUT bullet: 'the probot/dco GitHub app (it watches PRs, which we bot-close — dead config)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'which we bot-close — dead config' with 'Gerrit server-side enforcement is the authoritative gate; even if PRs remain open on the mirror, the probot/dco app only gates GitHub PRs and adds no value when enforcement is in Gerrit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the unverified 'Kubernetes-2026 norm' citation with a verifiable reference (e.g., the actual Kubernetes contributor guide URL, a CNCF DCO FAQ, or a specific commit in the Kubernetes repo that documents bot sign-off practice), and resolve the 'exact form' of the RebarBotNava `Signed-off-by` trailer in the plan rather than deferring it.
findings.finding: The plan invokes 'the Kubernetes-2026 norm' as the authoritative prior art for the bot/agent sign-off policy ('the responsible HUMAN signs off; AI is never a co-author'), but no published 'Kubernetes-2026' standard or KEP exists as a codified reference. The actual Kubernetes contributor guidance for bot commits as of 2025 is informal practice (bots use `[bot]` username suffixes; human committers add `Signed-off-by`), not a published 2026 specification. Citing an unverified future-dated norm as the canonical source for an implementation decision leaves the agent sign-off policy without a grounded reference, which matters because the 'exact form' of the bot trailer is deferred to implementation.
findings.location: What section — 'Agent/bot commits — adopt the Kubernetes-2026 norm'; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Cite the Kubernetes contributor guide at https://www.kubernetes.dev/docs/guide/pull-requests/#commit-message-guidelines or the CNCF DCO policy, and specify the exact trailer form inline, e.g. `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joe@navateam.com>` (the responsible human's registered identity), documenting that RebarBotNava is the git author while the human is the sign-off principal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the Gerrit documentation section that confirms `receive.requireSignedOffBy` is a valid per-project `project.config` key (not a silently-ignored global-only key like `change.submitWholeTopic`), and note whether it must also appear in `gerrit.config` for global enforcement vs. being project-scoped.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` belongs in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`, but does not resolve whether this key is a per-project setting (valid in `project.config`) or a server-global setting (which would need to live in `gerrit.config`, like `submitWholeTopic`). The existing codebase explicitly documents this distinction — `gerrit.config` comments on `submitWholeTopic` warn 'silently ignored if placed in a project's project.config' — yet the plan does not cite a Gerrit documentation reference confirming `receive.requireSignedOffBy` is per-project-safe. This is a verifiable capability assertion left unresolved in the plan.
findings.location: What section — 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note referencing Gerrit's configuration reference (https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/config-gerrit.html#receive.requireSignedOffBy) confirming it is a per-project key settable in `project.config`, and also flag whether the existing `[receive]` block in `infra/compose/gerrit.config` needs a parallel global entry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (via Gerrit 3.14.x documentation or a dry-run probe against the live instance) whether `receive.requireSignedOffBy` is enforced at the project level via `project.config` or only at the site level via `gerrit.config`, and update the plan's implementation location accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan places `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`, but the codebase's existing `infra/compose/gerrit.config` already has a `[receive]` section (for `enableSignedPush`), and Gerrit documentation treats `receive.requireSignedOffBy` as a key that can live at either scope. There is no prior usage of this key in the repo, so it is unverified whether the project-level `project.config [receive]` placement is sufficient to enforce rejection at push time, or whether it must instead live in the site-level `gerrit.config`. If the key is only effective site-wide, placing it in `project.config` would silently do nothing and the AC ('unsigned push rejected') would pass verification on a false positive.
findings.location: What section: 'Set `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` in `project.config` on `refs/meta/config`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line verification step to the migration plan: before setting the flag in production, test it in a scratch Gerrit project or confirm against the [Gerrit documentation for the installed version](https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/config-gerrit.html#receive.requireSignedOffBy) that project-level `project.config [receive]` enforcement is supported. If site-level, the stanza goes in `infra/compose/gerrit.config` under `[receive]` and requires a Gerrit restart/reload, not a `refs/meta/config` push.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the Gerrit server-side enforcement decision (vs. CI/GitHub-app alternatives, rationale, trade-offs) in a repo-resident durable artifact — an ADR file or a `## DCO enforcement` section in CLAUDE.md — not only on the ticket.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decision to enforce DCO at the Gerrit server layer (vs. CI-based enforcement or GitHub app) is recorded only on the ticket, not in a durable artifact such as an ADR or CLAUDE.md design-decisions section. Ticket notes are ephemeral and not co-located with the codebase, so future maintainers lack discoverable rationale for why enforcement is at `refs/for/*` push time rather than a CI gate.
findings.location: ## What — 'Record the CLA decision: none unless Nava counsel requires…' and Scope/IN section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/decisions/0001-dco-gerrit-server-enforcement.md` ADR (or an equivalent section in CLAUDE.md) covering: context (no inbound-IP mechanism), decision (server-side Gerrit flag), alternatives considered (probot/dco GitHub app, CI check), consequences (zero new infrastructure, pre-review rejection, agent-commit compliance requirement).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a single canonical location (e.g., a config constant or a dedicated `bot-sign-off.txt` / `.github/bot-dco.env` file) for the bot email and responsible-human identity, and have CLAUDE.md, automation scripts, and any other consumers reference or import from it rather than duplicating the value.
findings.finding: The agent/bot sign-off convention — specifically the 'exact form decided and documented here' for bot email and human-sign-off pairing — is left to be embedded as narrative prose in CLAUDE.md/CLAUDE.local.md. If the responsible human changes, the bot email is rotated, or the Kubernetes-2026 norm evolves, every file containing the convention must be located and updated manually with no single source of truth.
findings.location: ## What — 'Agent/bot commits — adopt the Kubernetes-2026 norm … exact form decided and documented here'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
